text: Artificial intelligence is changing many parts of our lives, from healthcare to finance. However, developing AI responsibly means thinking carefully about ethics. One major issue is bias. If the data used to train an AI system contains human prejudices, the AI may make unfair decisions, for example against certain groups of people. This is why fairness must be a priority. Another concern is privacy. AI systems often collect large amounts of personal information, so rules must protect users' data. Furthermore, transparency is essential; people should understand how an AI reaches its conclusions. Ultimately, responsible AI development requires a balance between innovation and ethical principles.
